data.title: Moody's changes China's outlook to stable from negative; affirms A1 rating
data.description:
Moody's Ratings affirmed the government of China's local and foreign-currency issuer and senior unsecured ratings at A1 and changed its outlook to stable from negative, the ratings firm said Monday.

"The stabilization of the outlook reflects our assessment that economic and fiscal strength will be resilient to ongoing domestic as well as trade and geopolitical challenges," Moody's said in a statement. It also said the outlook is supported by sustained growth and effective debt management, despite domestic and external challenges.

The ratings company expects the country's export growth to moderate, but its competitiveness and the resilience of its exports to rapid changes in the global trade picture will only slow GDP growth gradually in the medium term. At the same time, government policies that prioritize investment in high-productivity sectors while managing supply imbalances will improve capital efficiency, Moody's said.

Furthermore, China's policymakers are expected to manage the debt resolution process for regional and local governments in a controlled fashion while general government debt rises due to substantial policy support to the economy.

Moody's estimates China's real GDP growth at 4.5% in 2026 and 4.2% in 2027. Its debt is projected to rise to 82.4% in 2027 from 68.5% in 2025 and above 90% by the end of the decade.

The Chinese yuan rose 0.2% against the U.S. dollar in morning trading, New York time. The State Street SPDR China ETF (GXC) climbed 1.1%, the iShares MSCI China ETF (MCHI) edged down 0.2%, and the iShares China Large-Cap ETF (FXI) fell 0.8% in premarket trading.

data.title: HBT Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 beats by $0.06, revenue of $67.33M beats by $0.35M
data.description:
• HBT Financial press release (HBT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $67.33M (+16.1% Y/Y) beats by $0.35M.
  • Net interest margin increased 8 basis points to 4.20% and net interest margin (tax-equivalent basis) increased 9 basis points to 4.25%

data.title: CareTrust buys $628M in healthcare properties
data.description:
CareTrust REIT (CTRE) on Monday said it closed about $628M of investments in April, which includes the acquisition of 15 skilled nursing facilities in California.

The company said the California transaction totaled about $380M, including costs, and covered roughly 1700 beds.

The properties were leased back to affiliates of the seller under a long-term triple-net lease. CareTrust also originated two related loans totaling $163M secured by skilled nursing portfolios in California and Washington.

CareTrust acquired four care homes in the U.K. for about £42M and said it expects to buy a fifth property for about £9M after approvals. It also bought a 124-bed skilled nursing facility in Wyoming for about $20M.

The investments carry a blended stabilized yield of 8.8% and were funded using a combination of proceeds received from settled equity forward contracts and borrowings under its revolving credit facility, the company said.

Including the latest deals, CareTrust said its 2026 year-to-date investments total about $990M, with a near-term pipeline of about $450M.

Separately, Moody's Ratings assigned the company and its senior unsecured notes an investment-grade Baa3 rating with a stable outlook.

data.title: BASF hikes prices again for additives in plastic applications on Middle East war disruption
data.description:
BASF (BASFY) said Monday it is raising prices for products within its global portfolio of antioxidants, process stabilizers, and light stabilizers for plastic applications, citing substantial increases in raw material prices, energy, and logistic costs due to the Middle East war.

BASF (BASFY) said it will boost prices for the plastic-protecting chemicals, commonly used in the car and consumer goods industries, by as much as 25% effective immediately, in addition to a 20% hike announced in early March.

The products impacted are used to prolong the lifespan of plastics in various materials, including those used to make wire and cabling insulation, steering wheels, car door trims, and outdoor plastic furniture.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Verizon raises 2026 EPS outlook to 5-6%; phone adds seen high end
United's CEO explains why he pitched for a merger with American Airlines
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) CEO Scott Kirby confirmed that he approached American Airlines (AAL) about a merger over the last two weeks. Kirby said the goal was to pitch that a combination of the two major U.S. carriers would still be great for customers. However, he noted that American (AAL) declined to engage and instead responded by publicly closing the door.

"The bold idea I wanted to pursue was about growth that would usher in a brand new era of leadership by U.S. aviation. After all, flight was born here, and the storied names of the past, including both United and American, set the standards that the rest of the world aspired to. By combining our airlines and using that scale to revolutionize our customers' experience, we'd create a new, thriving U.S. airline that would be the very best in the world for customers," updated Kirby.

In simple terms, Kirby highlighted that combining United (UAL) and American (AAL) could unlock incredible, new opportunities for both airlines' customers, employees, and the communities served. He said a great, new U.S. airline would have the scale to compete and lead around the globe.

While United's (UAL) pursuit of talks with American (AAL) has ended, the mission to build the greatest airline in the history of aviation at United is well underway, added Kirby.

Shares of United Airlines (UAL) are up 34.7% over the last 52 weeks, while American Airlines (AAL) is 25.9% higher. The Dow Jones U.S. Airlines Index is 40.0% higher over the same period. Notably, Southwest Airlines (LUV) has the highest Seeking Alpha Quant rating of any global airline stock.

data.title: Lakeland Financial GAAP EPS of $1.04 beats by $0.03, revenue of $69.71M misses by $0.71M
data.description:
• Lakeland Financial press release (LKFN): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.04 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $69.71M (+9.3% Y/Y) misses by $0.71M.
  • Return on average equity improved to 13.89%, compared to 11.70%
  • Return on average assets improved to 1.52%, compared to 1.20%
  • Tangible book value per share grew by $2.84, or 11%, to $29.69
  • Average loans grew by $255.0 million, or 5%, to $5.44 billion
  • Average deposits grew by $180.8 million, or 3%, to $6.06 billion
  • Net interest margin improved 9 basis points to 3.49% versus 3.40%
  • Net interest income increased by $3.9 million, or 7%
  • Noninterest income increased by $2.0 million, or 18%

data.title: KBR wins $200M U.S. transportation technology contract
data.description:
• KBR (KBR) on Monday said it won a recompete contract worth up to $200M to help enhance aviation safety and modernize U.S. transportation networks in partnership with the Department of Transportation’s Volpe National Transportation Systems Center.
  • The five-year agreement, known as TTEMS, is a single-award blanket purchase agreement issued through the General Services Administration, which manages the federal government's real estate portfolio.
  • Under the contract, KBR will provide engineering services, artificial intelligence, machine learning, modeling and simulation, data analytics, and information technology support in Cambridge, Massachusetts, and other locations worldwide.
  • The work will support multiple transportation agencies, including the Federal Aviation Administration, National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, and Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration.

data.title: Alliance Resource Partners GAAP EPS of $0.07 misses by $0.08, revenue of $516M misses by $1.81M
data.description:
• Alliance Resource Partners press release (ARLP): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.07 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $516M (-4.5% Y/Y) misses by $1.81M.
  • 2026 expected coal sales volumes over 95% committed and priced at the midpoint of 2026 guidance
  • Record oil & gas royalty revenues and volumes, up 14.6% and 16.1%, respectively, year-over-year
  • Completed $16.2 million in oil & gas mineral interest acquisitions during the 2026 Quarter
  • Total and net leverage ratios as of March 31, 2026, were 0.73 times and 0.69 times, respectively
  • ARLP announced today that the Board of Directors of ARLP’s general partner approved a cash distribution to unitholders for the 2026 Quarter of $0.60 per unit (an annualized rate of $2.40 per unit), payable on May 15, 2026, to all unitholders of record as of the close of trading on May 8, 2026.
  • Shares +3.2% PM.

data.title: Fulcrum Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.25 beats by $0.05
data.description:
